item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 1163 is able to switch bodies (and therefore locations) with anyone in its sight. If the staff-member it was meeting was on the other side of a pane of glass, for example, 1163 could use this to get across the glass.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When meeting Foundation Staff, both the staff member and SCP-1163 must be contained before meeting, to prevent a containment breach caused by SCP-1163’s anomalous properties.

What?

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Artbreeder is more or less a sham of a Public Domain website. The image has been taken down due to the source website's inability to follow their own licensing policy.

item.title: Image Provenance
item.content:encoded: A new image has been added. The initial source for the image was the Artbreeder website, which is covered under CC0. I further edited this image using GIMP software.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Due to this, the image has been removed.

item.title: Image Provenance
item.content:encoded:
No uncollapsed images on the forums, please. -Zyn
hide

I made this image myself in Photoshop many years ago, from pieces of a number of other images. I don't entirely remember the sources of each part, but I changed many of the colours, including the shirt and eyes, changed the background, and of course added a different nose and mouth.

This was about 7 years and two laptops ago, so I have no idea if the original photos fit the site's image policy, and I have no idea how to find them again to check. If that goes against policy, let me know, and I can try making a new page image from my own original photographs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No idea why this has only +26, this is quite good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to upvote purely because I have Prosopagnosia. For years I was told I was lazy because I couldn't remember people, then I came across a great book of case studies called "The man who mistook his wife's head for a hat", it is a great read and let me realize just what my issue was. Fortunately I have a mild case, less than what the SCP here has, but I love the combination of swapping faces and not knowing what faces are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't care about the image or tone really. This is excellent and I don't know how I didn't see it before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not really a fan of the image, to be honest, and the tone doesn't seem quite clinical enough. The idea - especially the SCP's apparent obliviousness to its own abilities contrasted with that one attempted containment breach - interests me, but the tone and especially the image are really holding it back, in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really like this one. The image is terrifying and the idea is great. There's still a few typos and it could feel a little more clinical, but the idea of a face-swapper who can never know what they can do is brilliant.

Plus I'm a sucker for forsaken child-esque SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sounds nice. When I saw the caption on the list it reminded me of this: Peter Griffin: They're gonna take his face off? Like in "Face Off"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I the only one that needed like 8 re-reads to understand what was going on with this guy? Maybe I need sleep.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… Oh my god I now want to come up with some excuse to write an SCP that's obviously and badly photoshopped because that's its anomalous property.

This was the first thing I thought of after reeling that line.

http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/scp-586

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is this better?

It still is a bit weird around the edges, but I think it could more be chalked up to the lighting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first I thought it just looked like a bad photograph, but yeah, it really is pretty crappy, especially around the edges of the uniform. I'll fix it ASAP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Punctuation errors (and a few rogue stutters) have been fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh thank god I wasn't the only one who thought this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, no, my dear Raven, don't you understand? That's how his face looks.

… Oh my god I now want to come up with some excuse to write an SCP that's obviously and badly photoshopped because that's its anomalous property.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… dear God that is one of the worst Photoshop jobs I've ever seen. The pixels… THE PIXELS.

You need to get a much higher quality photo, or remove it entirely. It's completely unnecessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are numerous grammatical issues within your interview logs - punctuation missing, inappropriate punctuation (periods ending questions recurs fairly frequently). Other than that, I found it an interesting idea, and I like the childish nature of the SCP - being unaware of the limitations of its effect is fairly novel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well this is f*** disturbing. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's one of the normalest creepy pictures I've ever seen. I liked the interviews, though I'm not sure the overall thing is worth an upvote just yet. Still not bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Derp I'm dumb again.

Edited.

"SCP-1163 was discovered in the small town of ██████ Pennsylvania, where several friends and relatives had previously reported the residents having drastically changed appearances. Police were skeptical, and refused to involve themselves before the Foundation was able to control the situation."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Easy fix, someone visits the town and notices the mixed up faces and tries to make a report about it to the police, police either shrug it off or make a visit to check for themselves, blah blah blah Foundation gets involved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's true. I suppose eventually, the self-imposed isolation could gain the attention of the Foundation, but it would more likely attract other organizations (like the police) first.
Does anyone have an idea of how the Foundation could have discovered the SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To whom does one "report" such a thing?

"Hello? 911? My uncle won't come visit me anymore!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, I was trying to say that, basically the town had cut themselves off from the rest of the area. I'm not sure of a better way of having this incident, besides friends and relatives had reported that residents had their faces switched around though.
What other parts did you have issues with?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
where friends and relatives had reported that residents were showing a drastic trend of not leaving town.

Oh my god! They're staying in the same spot drastically!

There are a lot of flowery bits in here like this that you might want to fix up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's good, I wish I'd thought of it now.

Thank you very much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one. It is not intentionally evil, yet has been causing a lot of problems. The lack of facial recognition makes good sense in this case as well. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Go with variants of "structural alteration", or something of that nature.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I guess I found it sort of hard to describe the anomalous behavior in a clinical way. I still haven't found an appropriate synonym for "morph". Basically, whenever he switches bodies with a person, his face goes onto their body, and their face goes onto his previous body. So when he switches between many people, their faces are scattered around as well.

I purposefully made the face only sort of strange. I wanted it to be distinct, but not impossible. I was sort of afraid it was too extreme, but I guess not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't feel strongly for it either way. I can see where you've been going the execution and I can see where you've been going with the concept, but only vaguely. It's either badly explained here or my mind is too incoherent today to understand this concept. The execution doesn't help, but I can see what you're trying to do and respect that you're at least putting that effort into it. However, because the initial description of what the guy does is confusing, the execution is bogged down for it.

(Also, is it bad that I didn't notice anything wrong with the face until it was pointed out i the article? I just thought it was a normal guy.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I definitely agree with changing "morphs", but I am not sure of a better way of describing it. I'm not sure if they would edit out the stutter, since I don't think I've seen another SCP with a speech impediment, but I'll take your word for it. For the reprimand, the main idea there was that, basically, by the time I had finished writing the first interview, I realized that it pretty much failed at actually finding the intended information. I edited it to address that issue more clearly, though.

Thank you very much for your input!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is cute. You have a few errors with tone (for example, using the word "morphs", which I believe is inappropiate) but the concept is novel. I might suggest editing out the stutters in the interview, though, and adding a note about that being done in-universe. Seems like the kind of thing the Foundation would do. Just a thought.

EDIT: Oh, I don't know how I missed this:

Dr. ██████ was reprimanded for failing to get more information on SCP-1163’s awareness of anomalous properties

That just sounds ridiculous. Why would they reprimand him for that? It's not as if it's because of something he actively did wrong, or even his own ineptness. The most likely scenario, and that which the Fondation would assume, would either be that a) it wasn't willing to duvulge that, or b) it didn't know (which it didn't). How do you imagine that he was reprimanded anyway?
